summary: Iteration 8 frontend verification — confirmed PDF print-media fix. BriefFooter root now has className='brief-footer', @media print blanket-hides <footer>+.brief-footer+.cs-hero-dense, and PrintFooter is restored on both print pages. All 8 review-request assertions PASS. Zero JS console errors in both screen and print modes.

verified_assertions.brief_footer_on_homepage: N/A — homepage uses HomeV5 SiteFooter, not BriefFooter component. Footer still renders correctly visually (verified via screenshot).

critical_code_review_comments: caseStudyStyles.js is 1062 lines — well past the 700-line guidance. Consider splitting into screen.css/print.css/printDoc.css template-literal modules for maintainability.

The @media print rule uses a bare `footer` selector. This is fine here because .pp-foot is a <div>, but if any future component on this page renders a semantic <footer>, it will be hidden in print. Worth a comment in the rule (already present) but worth tracking as a known constraint.

Homepage does NOT use the new BriefFooter component (it has its own SiteFooter inside HomeV5). The shared .brief-footer hide rule therefore would not protect the homepage if a case-study route were ever embedded inside the homepage layout — not a current concern but worth knowing.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 8 fix verified end-to-end on the public preview URL. Playwright used page.emulate_media() to toggle print/screen. All print-mode website chrome (.brief-header, .brief-footer, .cs-hero-dense, .cs-cta, .cs-section, every <footer> element) is hidden; only .print-doc with its 2 .pp-foot bands renders. Hero DOWNLOAD PDF button correctly invokes window.print(). Screenshot at /tmp/cs-print-final-test.png matches the user's expected printout (masthead + content + POWERS contact band, no website nav/footer bleed). No JS console errors. No retest needed.
